title: Are you willing to sink further? Are you ferociously obsessed? — two-question check
html:
Are you willing to sink further? Are you ferociously obsessed? — two-question check

Source: 2025-12-02 Apple Note. Two questions, second-person addressed to himself. Written ~3 weeks before the Rankett ops questions and one month into the Rankett sales-push period.

TL;DR

  • "Are you willing to sinker further and go deeper? Are you ferociously obsessed?" — the entire note, two diagnostic questions back-to-back.
  • Sister specimen to the Nov 20 "uncommon amongst uncommon" line — same gym-scratchpad register, same manifesto style note genre, written 12 days later.
  • The first question (sink further / go deeper) implies Yahir already feels deep — the question is whether the depth can compound.
  • The second question (ferociously obsessed) borrows the Silicon-Valley founder vocabulary directly — ferocious obsession as the binary qualifier for category-founder ambition.
  • Pure note as letter to self mode — not a directive, a check-in template Yahir can re-read whenever he questions his own intensity.

Key quote

"Are you ferociously obsessed?"

Pattern observed

This is the interrogative variant of the manifesto — instead of declaring identity ("I am a killer") or instructing self ("be uncommon"), Yahir asks. Same mechanism, different surface: forcing a yes/no answer in the moment of re-reading.

The pairing — willingness to sink + ferocity of obsession — captures both the voluntary direction (voluntary adversity) and the involuntary intensity sides of founder commitment. Yahir wants both confirmed. The question structure suggests doubt was creeping in mid-Rankett-build period; the manifesto-as-question is the response.

Open questions

  • What was the trigger? (Mid-Rankett pivot from build to sales? Diddles partner-pitch prep?)
  • Did Yahir answer these questions in a follow-up note, or are they left as open prompts?
  • "Ferociously obsessed" — borrowed from a specific founder quote, or self-coined?
